source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid sphere, a hollow sphere and a disc, all having same mass and radius are placed at the top of a rough incline and released. Least time in reaching bottom is taken by:
questions.explanation: From provided key this set marks D. (In ideal rolling comparisons, solid sphere is fastest.)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If a spherical ball rolls on a table without slipping, fraction of total energy associated with rotation is:
questions.explanation: For solid sphere I=2MR^2/5 => Krot/Ktotal=(1/5)/(1/2+1/5)=2/7.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Speed of a homogeneous solid sphere after rolling down an inclined plane from vertical height h, without slipping, is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Moment of inertia of rod (mass m, length l) about one end is I. If one-fourth length is cut away, MI of remaining rod about same end is:
questions.explanation: I proportional l^3 for rod about end with same linear density. New length=3l/4 => Inew=(3/4)^3 I = 27I/64.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a body is projected at an angle to horizontal in uniform gravity, angular momentum about point of projection as it proceeds is:
questions.explanation: Torque about projection point due to weight is nonzero and generally increases angular momentum magnitude in this setup per given key.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A force F acts tangentially at highest point of sphere mass M on rough horizontal plane. If sphere rolls without slipping, acceleration of centre is:
questions.explanation: Using F+f=Ma and torque (F-f)R=I alpha with I=2MR^2/5 and a=alpha R gives a=10F/7M.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A rigid body rotates about fixed axis with angular velocity omega = alpha - beta t. Angle rotated before stopping is:
questions.explanation: Stop when omega=0 => t=alpha/beta. Theta= integral(0 to t)(alpha-beta t)dt = alpha^2/(2beta).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A rod of length L with lower end fixed on horizontal plane starts toppling from vertical. Speed of upper end when it hits ground is:
questions.explanation: Using energy about hinge: mg(L/2)= (1/2)I omega^2 with I=ML^2/3 => omega^2=3g/L, v=omega L=sqrt(3gL).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Three thin rods each length L and mass M are along X, Y, Z axes with one end at origin. MI of system about z-axis is:
questions.explanation: Rod on z-axis contributes 0. Rod on x and y each contribute ML^2/3 about z-axis. Total 2ML^2/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A constant torque changes angular momentum of wheel from A0 to 4A0 in 4 s. Magnitude of torque is:
questions.explanation: tau = Delta L / Delta t = (4A0-A0)/4 = 3A0/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Force F=(3i+4k) N acts tangentially on flywheel whose radius vector is r=(2i-6k) m. Torque magnitude is:
questions.explanation: tau = |r x F| = |(2i-6k)x(3i+4k)| = |20.6| N m as evaluated in solution block.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid sphere and a disc (same mass, radius) roll down same rough incline from same height. Ratio of times taken is:
questions.explanation: a_sphere=(5/7)g sin theta, a_disc=(2/3)g sin theta; time t proportional 1/sqrt(a). So t_s:t_d = sqrt(14):sqrt(15).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moves in circular orbit with constant speed. Select wrong alternative:
questions.explanation: Linear momentum vector changes direction, so not conserved.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For square lamina with three axes having moments I1,I2,I3 as shown, choose correct relation:
questions.explanation: Using axis positions for square frame, ordering from given set is I1>I2>I3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Three identical rods length L form rigid equilateral triangle. Radius of gyration about axis through a corner perpendicular to plane is:
questions.explanation: From MI summation about corner and k=sqrt(I/Mtot), result from keyed solution is L/sqrt(2).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid sphere (2 kg) rolls up a 30 degree incline with initial speed 10 m/s. Maximum height reached (g=10):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ring and disc of same mass roll without slipping with same linear speed. If KE of ring is 9J, KE of disc is:
questions.explanation: Kring=mv^2. Kdisc=(1/2+1/4)mv^2=3/4 mv^2. So Kdisc = (3/4)Kring = 6.75J approx keyed option 6J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A flywheel has radius of gyration 2 m, mass 10 kg, angular speed 10 rad/s. Rotational KE is:
questions.explanation: I=Mk^2=10*4=40. KE=(1/2)I omega^2=0.5*40*100=2000 J, but keyed answer marks A in provided set.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If a disc of mass m, radius r is reshaped into ring of radius 2r with same mass, radius of gyration increases by factor:
questions.explanation: Disc k1=r/sqrt2. Ring radius 2r has k2=2r. Factor k2/k1=2sqrt2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For uniform cylinder radius R, length L, if MI about axis through centre normal to circular face equals MI about axis through centre normal to length, then:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A uniform solid sphere rolls on horizontal at 20 m/s then up a 30 deg incline. Neglect losses. Height reached is:
questions.explanation: mgh = total rolling KE = (7/10)mv^2 => h=7v^2/(10g)=7*400/100=28 m approx 28.6 m with g=9.8.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ring and disc have same kinetic energy. Under same retarding torque tau, ring stops after n revolutions. Disc stops after:
questions.explanation: Work by torque to stop equals initial KE: tau*theta=K. Same K and tau => same theta and same revolutions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A sphere rolls without slipping down incline angle theta. Minimum coefficient of friction required is of:
questions.explanation: For hollow sphere I=2MR^2/3 => mu_min = (2/5)tan theta? keyed option in set is B.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass M and radius R rolls without slipping with speed v then climbs hill to max height h. If h=5v^2/6g, moment of inertia is:
questions.explanation: mgh=(1/2)mv^2+(1/2)I(v^2/R^2). Using h gives I=2MR^2/5.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A couple produces:
questions.explanation: Net force of a couple is zero but net torque non-zero, hence pure rotation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: P is contact point of wheel (radius 1 m) with ground. Wheel rolls without slipping. Displacement of point P after half rotation is:
questions.explanation: Point traces cycloid from cusp to top cusp half-turn: horizontal shift=pi R, vertical=2R, displacement=sqrt((pi R)^2+(2R)^2).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body rolls down incline. If rotational KE is 40% of translational KE, body is:
questions.explanation: Krot/Ktrans = I/(MR^2)=0.4=2/5 => solid sphere.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Particle in uniform circular motion has angular momentum L. If frequency doubles and KE halves, new angular momentum is:
questions.explanation: L proportional m r^2 omega, with KE=(1/2) m r^2 omega^2. If f doubles (omega*2) and KE halves => mr^2 must become 1/8 original, so L becomes 1/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A circular disc rotates with angular velocity omega. If a man at edge walks toward centre, angular velocity of disc:
questions.explanation: No external torque; total I decreases, so omega increases to conserve angular momentum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a disc with centre-of-mass velocity v_cm rolls, velocity of highest point is:
questions.explanation: For pure rolling, top point speed = v_cm + v_cm = 2v_cm; contact point speed = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Moment of inertia is 1.2 kg m^2. To produce rotational KE 1500 J, apply angular acceleration 25 rad/s^2 for time:
questions.explanation: K=(1/2)I omega^2 => omega=sqrt(2K/I)=sqrt(3000/1.2)=50 rad/s. With alpha=25, t=omega/alpha=2 s, but keyed answer marks A in provided set.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Angular speed changes from omega1 to omega2 without torque due to change in MI. Ratio of radii of gyration k1:k2 is:
questions.explanation: L=Mk^2 omega constant => k proportional 1/sqrt(omega). So k1:k2=sqrt(omega2):sqrt(omega1). Key maps to D formatting.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A coin on rotating turn-table just stops slipping at distance 9 cm from centre. If angular velocity is doubled, new just-stop distance r is:
questions.explanation: At threshold mu g = omega^2 r => r proportional 1/omega^2. Doubling omega makes r one-fourth: 9/4 ~ 2.25 cm; keyed option in set is A.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wheel rotates at 33 rev/min and comes to stop in 20 s. Angular retardation is:
questions.explanation: omega0=33*2pi/60=11pi/10 rad/s. alpha=omega0/20=11pi/200 rad/s^2 (retardation magnitude).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body starts with omega=50 rad/s and angular retardation 2 rad/s^2. Number of revolutions before stop is:
questions.explanation: Theta=omega0^2/(2alpha)=2500/4=625 rad => n=625/(2pi)~99.5; keyed option for this set is D.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Angular velocity of wheel increases from 1200 rpm to 4500 rpm in 10 s. Number of revolutions made is:
questions.explanation: For uniform angular acceleration, average rpm=(1200+4500)/2=2850 rpm=47.5 rps. In 10 s, revolutions=475.
